     The Numbers Business Report

Steve Jobs (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $17,766,658Details
International Box Office $17,812,349
Worldwide Box Office $35,579,007
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,335,159
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,724,446
Total $3,059,605
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Set backstage in the minutes before three iconic product launches spanning Jobs’ career—beginning with the Macintosh in 1984, and ending with the unveiling of the iMac in 1998—this film takes us behind the scenes of the digital revolution to paint a portrait of the brilliant man at its epicenter.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$521,522 (2.9% of total gross)
Legs:2.50 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:49.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/2,493 max. theaters, 2.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $25,206,310

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 9th, 2015 (Limited) by Universal
October 23rd, 2015 (Expands Wide) by Universal
International Releases: November 12th, 2015 (Wide) (Czech Republic)
November 12th, 2015 (Wide) (Austria)
November 12th, 2015 (Wide) (United Arab Emirates)
November 12th, 2015 (Wide) (Ukraine)
November 12th, 2015 (Wide) (Switzerland)
Video Release: February 2nd, 2016 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language.
(Rating bulletin 2387, 8/19/2015)
Running Time: 121 minutes
Plot point: Biography, Entrepreneur, Inventor
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Time period setting: 1980s, 1990s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Scott Rudin Productions, Entertainment 360, The Mark Gordon Company, Decibel Films, Cloud Eight
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Set backstage in the minutes before three iconic product launches spanning Jobs’ career—beginning with the Macintosh in 1984, and ending with the unveiling of the iMac in 1998—this film takes us behind the scenes of the digital revolution to paint a portrait of the brilliant man at its epicenter.
